channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Sokoto LG chairman quits APC, resigns from office
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Tangaza Local Government Area chairman, Isa Kalanjeni, has resigned from the APC and stepped down from his position in Sokoto State due to personal and adm
Read More: https://punchng.com/sokoto-lg-chairman-quits-apc-resigns-from-office/

item.title: Kwankwaso knocks ex-Borno gov over comments on Peter Obi’s northern support
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Rabiu Kwankwaso criticises Ali Modu Sheriff’s comments on Peter Obi’s electoral strength in Northern Nigeria, citing Obi’s 2.8 million votes.
Read More: https://punchng.com/kwankwaso-knocks-ex-borno-gov-over-comments-on-peter-obis-northern-support/

item.title: UK party accuses Kemi Badenoch of running scared over MP Farage’s resignation
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Reform UK accuses Kemi Badenoch of
Read More: https://punchng.com/uk-party-accuses-kemi-badenoch-of-running-scared-over-mp-farages-resignation/

item.title: Tinubu names Gbajabiamila, AGF, others in state police implementation committee
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu forms a committee, led by Gbajabiamila, to draft the National Policing Bill for state police implementation in Nigeria, addressing securit
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-names-gbajabiamila-agf-others-in-state-police-implementation-committee/

item.title: Mexican national dies after ICE agent shooting in Texas
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Mexican national died after an ICE agent shooting in Texas during an attempted vehicle stop. The incident is under investigation by the FBI.
Read More: https://punchng.com/mexican-national-dies-after-ice-agent-shooting-in-texas/

item.title: UN blames economic hardship, not feminism, for declining global fertility
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A new UN report reveals economic hardship, not feminism or selfishness, is the primary cause of declining global fertility rates, challenging common assump
Read More: https://punchng.com/un-blames-economic-hardship-not-feminism-for-declining-global-fertility/

item.title: Russell: I need to be better to fight for championship
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: George Russell has admitted he needs to be better if he is going to stay in title contention this season.

item.title: Oil Jumps Following US Strikes on Iran Targets (Video)
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets

item.title: Court grants Peller bail after four days in police detention
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A Magistrate's Court in Lagos has granted bail to Nigerian TikToker Peller in a case involving the alleged obstruction of police officers. The post Court grants Peller bail after four days in police detention appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Does our family deserve this after campaigning for you – El-Rufai’s wife asks Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Hajiya Asiya El-Rufai, wife of former Kaduna State governor, Nasir El-Rufai, has appealed to President Bola Ahmed Tinubu to ensure her husband receives what she described as his constitutional rights while facing prosecution. Speaking to journalists in Abuja on Tuesday alongside El-Rufai’s sec

item.title: NNPC remits N19.5tn to Federation Account in 14 months
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Obas Esiedesa, Abuja The Group Chief Executive Officer of NNPC Limited, Engr. Bayo Ojulari, has disclosed that the company remitted N19.5 trillion to the Federation Account between April 2025 and June 2026. Speaking at the 2026 Nigeria Oil and Gas Energy Week in Abuja on Tuesday, Ojulari said the

item.title: PFIPC scandal: Family members of criminal suspect can’t be arrested – Falana on Adeyemi’s father
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Human rights lawyer and activist, Femi Falana, SAN, has informed the Tinubu administration that it contravenes the constitution of the Federal Republic for an individual to be detained in place of a suspect. This statement follows the lawyer’s earlier revelation that police had arrested the fa

item.title: OFFICIAL: Iwobi, Bassey To Play Under Arbeloa At Fulham
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nigeria internationals Calvin Bassey and Alex Iwobi have a new manager at Fulham following the appointment of Alvaro Arbeloa, Sky Sports reports. The ex-Real Madrid boss has signed a three-year deal at Craven Cottage to become Marco Silva’s successor after the Portuguese left for Benfica follo

item.title: Official: Akpe Victory joins 21-time Swiss champions Basel
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Former Swiss champions Basel have announced the signing of Nigerian defender Akpe Victory from Hungarian club Zalaegerszeg, Soccernet.ng reports. ​Victory has been the subject of significant transfer speculation over the last two months. The 19-year-old center-back only joined Zalaegerszeg from Bang

item.title: World Cup: Kyle Walker, Micah Richards name only team that’ll stop England from winning trophy
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former England internationals, Kyle Walker and Micah Richards have agreed that there is only one team capable of preventing the Three Lions from achieving a historic second World Cup victory. England secured their spot in the World Cup quarter-finals following a remarkable 3-2 victory over co-hosts

item.title: World Cup: ‘Egypt’s right has been violated’ – Mohamed Aboutreika slams ref, FIFA over Argentina’s win
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Egypt midfielder, Mohamed Aboutreika, has attributed his team’s 3-2 defeat to Lionel Messi’s Argentina in the 2026 Round of 16 in Atlanta on Tuesday to FIFA and the referees. The Pharaohs were in a favorable position, leading the defending champions by two goals midway t

item.title: Victorious Belgian footballers troll Trump with YMCA dance
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: It is Donald Trump's signature dance, but triumphant Belgian footballers stole his "Y.M.C.A." moves after he intervened before the match in which they knocked the United States out of the World Cup. The post Victorious Belgian footballers troll Trump with YMCA dance appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: France, Mbappe play Morocco in FIFA World Cup quarterfinal: What we know
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Key talking points as Kylian Mbappe leads France into a rematch after Les Bleus beat Morocco in the 2022 World Cup.

item.title: Cadbury, NAHCO, Dangote Cement lead NGX rally as investors gain N1.86 trillion
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigerian equities market sustained its bullish run on Tuesday, July 7, 2026, as broad-based buying interest across banking, industrial goods, consumer goods, oil and gas, and insurance sectors lifted the benchmark index for the second consecutive session, adding N1.86 trillion to investor wealth

item.title: South Africa: Madlanga Commission to Rule on Request for Private Testimony - South African News Briefs - July 8, 2026
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [allAfrica]

item.title: World Cup 2026: ‘Why Argentina’s winning goal against Egypt should’ve been disallowed’ – Ian Wright
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former England international, Ian Wright, has said that Argentina’s winning goal in their 3-2 victory over Egypt in the Round of 16 of the 2026 FIFA World Cup ought to have been ruled out. Wright, the ex-Arsenal forward, contended that Salah was fouled in the lead-up to the late goal scored by

item.title: Ondo: Politics of candidates’ substitution and power struggle
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: HAKEEM GBADAMOSI writes on how the consensus arrangement by the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Ondo State backfired, leading to the resolve by the National Working Committee (NWC) of the party to change the list which initially emanated from the state. The post Ondo: Politics of candidates’ subs

item.title: Inside Nigeria’s new blueprint to compete in the global digital services economy
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Tech
item.description: Nigeria is quietly building what officials believe could become one of its most valuable export industries outside oil, betting that read more Inside Nigeria’s new blueprint to compete in the global digital services economy

item.title: End this ‘psychological warfare’, El-Rufai’s wives beg Tinubu
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Asiya also claimed that the family of the former governor has been living in fear about what may happened to him in custody of ICPC because of his medical condition.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Wenger names team to win trophy ahead of quarter-final fixtures
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Legendary Arsenal manager, Arsene Wenger, is tipping France to win the 2026 World Cup. Wenger, believes that Les Bleus will appear in a third consecutive final, and will lift the trophy. Didier Deschamps’ side finished as Group I winners with a perfect record. Since then, they have eliminated Sweden

item.title: World Cup 2026: Hassan Slams Officiating In Egypt’s Loss To Argentina
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Egypt head coach Hossam Hassan has blamed controversial refereeing decisions for his team’s defeat to Argentina, Completesports.com reports. The Pharaohs crashed out of the FIFA World Cup 2026 after a thrilling 3-2 defeat to Lionel Scaloni’s side. The North Africans appeared on course for one of the

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Why I cried after Argentina eliminated Egypt – Messi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina captain, Lionel Messi, has revealed why he was emotional after they beat Egypt 3-2 to qualify for the quarter-finals of the 2026 World Cup on Tuesday night. Messi had the chance to make it 1-1 from the penalty spot after Nasser Ibrahim opened the scoring. But the 39-year-old saw

item.title: Zulum approves N1.65bn scholarship for 300 CJTF orphans, others
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Borno State Governor, Prof. Babagana Umara Zulum, has approved the release of N1.649 billion to settle outstanding scholarship obligations for 300 orphans of Counter-Insurgency Volunteers and other students. The Executive Secretary of the Borno State Scholarships Board, Dr Bala Isa, disclosed this d

item.title: Zazzau, Gombe emirs seek traditional rulers’ collaboration for national unity
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Emir of Gombe, Alhaji Abubakar Shehu Abubakar III, has called for stronger collaboration among traditional rulers to promote national unity, peace, security and cultural preservation. He made the call during a courtesy visit to the Emir of Zazzau, Mallam Ahmed Nuhu Bamalli, at his palace in Zari

item.title: ‘Vandalism threatens Nigeria’s fibre broadband expansion’
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of MegaMore Wireless Broadband Limited, Amin Dayekh, has identified vandalism, road construction activities and inadequate infrastructure protection as major threats to Nigeria’s fibre broadband expansion. Dayekh, in a statement by his spokesperson,

item.title: EFCC hands over 1,452 recovered hostel items to education ministry
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC), on Tuesday, handed over 1,452 items recovered from proceeds of crime to the Federal Ministry of Education to support schools across the country. The recovered items, comprising 501 double-step bunk beds, 939 mattresses and 12 wooden beds with matt

item.title: Tinubu tasks Afreximbank to mobilise resources for Africa’s industrialisation
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has urged the African Export-Import Bank (Afreximbank) to intensify efforts to mobilise Africa’s human, financial and natural resources to accelerate the continent’s industrialisation and economic growth. He made the call on Tuesday while receiving a delegation led by the

item.title: World Cup 2026: Why I Cried After Argentina’s Comeback Win Against Egypt
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni has disclosed that he was overcome with emotion and shed tears because he was deeply moved by his players’ relentless fighting spirit. Recall that Argentina staged a historic 3-2 comeback against Egypt in the FIFA World Cup 2026, fighting back from a 2-0 deficit

item.title: Adamawa launches solar mini-grid project for communities
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Adamawa State Government, in partnership with the Rural Electrification Agency (REA) and the Yola Electricity Distribution Company (YEDC), has launched the construction of solar hybrid interconnected mini-grids for three communities in the state. Speaking at the groundbreaking ceremony in Yola,

item.title: Insecurity: Northern traditional rulers, Islamic scholars meet in Kaduna
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Sultan of Sokoto, Alhaji Muhammad Sa’ad Abubakar III, on Tuesday led prominent traditional rulers and Islamic scholars from across Northern Nigeria to a high-level meeting of the Sultan Foundation for Peace and Development in Kaduna. The meeting, chaired by the Sultan, who is also Chairman of th

item.title: Policeman killed, 13 others injured in Taraba attacks
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A policeman was killed and three civilians injured in an attack by gunmen in Gembu, headquarters of the Sardauna Local Government Area of Taraba State. The attack occurred on Sunday, the same day a herder was killed and more than 10 farmers were injured during a clash between farmers and herders in

item.title: Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Wednesday
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Good morning to readers. Daily Sun brings you the major stories from Nigerian Newspapers from across the country. The post Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Wednesday appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Kidnappers demand fresh N260m from electoral commissioner’s family
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Hajiya Rahmat Ahmed, the wife of the kidnapped Permanent Commissioner of the Niger State Independent Electoral Commission (NSIEC), Barrister Ahmed Mohammed, has revealed that the abductors refused to collect the N40 million ransom raised by the family until it was converted to dollars. Barrister Moh

item.title: 2026 World Cup: All quarter-final fixtures confirmed
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The full quarter-final fixture list for the 2026 World Cup has now been confirmed. This follows the conclusion of the Round of 16 matches on Tuesday night. Switzerland became the last team to book their spot after they beat Colombia 4-3 on penalties. The match had ended 0-0 after 120 minut

item.title: 2 ‘top Boko Haram commanders’ surrender in Yobe
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Two senior Boko Haram commanders have surrendered to troops in the Geidam Local Government Area of Yobe State. The terrorists’ commanders, who surrendered to Sector 2, North East Joint Task Force Operation HADIN KAI (OPHK), have since arrived in Maiduguri. The acting media information officer of the

item.title: Police nab man with alleged stolen bull in Bauchi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Bauchi State Police Command has arrested a 22-year-old man allegedly found in possession of a stolen bull valued at N800,000. The suspect has been handed over to the Jigawa State Police Command for further investigation and possible prosecution. The suspect, identified as Shu’aibu Musa of

item.title: Egypt: Brave Egypt Fall Just Short As Argentina Stage Dramatic Comeback to Reach World Cup Quarter-Finals
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [CAF] Egypt's FIFA World Cup dream ended in heartbreaking fashion on Tuesday after the Pharaohs produced a heroic performance before eventually succumbing 3-2 to defending champions Argentina in a dramatic Round of 16 encounter in Atlanta.

item.title: Nigeria: Senate Condemns Rehabilitation of Repentant Boko Haram Terrorists, to Meet Tinubu Over Worsening Insecurity
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Premium Times] Nigeria's policy of rehabilitating and reintegrating repentant terrorists has remained one of the country's most controversial counterterrorism strategies.

item.title: PenCom raises PenCare eligibility to N150,000, eyes mortgage access for retirees
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The National Pension Commission (PenCom) has raised the income eligibility threshold for its PenCare Initiative from N70,000 to N150,000 monthly read more PenCom raises PenCare eligibility to N150,000, eyes mortgage access for retirees

item.title: China’s Deadly Storms Turbocharged by Convergence of Factors
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: This week’s deadly storms in southern and central China were worsened by a convergence of factors, underscoring the growing frequency of weather extremes that the world’s second-largest economy must increasingly contend with.

item.title: Tinubu seeks AFREXIM Bank’s support for infrastructure, industrialisation
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: President Bola Tinubu on Tuesday tasked the leadership of the African Export-Import Bank (Afreximbank) to intensify efforts to mobilise and read more Tinubu seeks AFREXIM Bank’s support for infrastructure, industrialisation

item.title: Egypt coach says team were ‘cheated’ in World Cup exit against Argentina
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Egypt head coach Hossam Hassan has claimed his team were "cheated" out of a place in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals after surrendering a two-goal lead to lose 3-2 to Argentina in a dramatic Round of 16 clash on Tuesday. The post Egypt coach says team were ‘cheated’ in World Cup exit a

item.title: ADC accuses ICPC of assaulting El-Rufai’s wife, arresting physician
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has accused the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) of assaulting the wife of former Kaduna State Governor, Nasir El-Rufai, and unlawfully arresting his personal physician during a hospital visit in Abuja. The post ADC accu

item.title: Kwankwaso fires back at Ali Modu Sheriff, affirms Peter Obi’s Northern support
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Vice-presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso, has dismissed claims by former Borno State Governor, Ali Modu Sheriff, that Labour Party presidential candidate Peter Obi lacks significant political support in Northern Nigeria. The remarks were contained in

item.title: Threading on dangerous grounds
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Sunny Ikhioya Stories coming out of the seat of power these days are disillusioning; it is as if we are playing games of pirates and vikings, superintending over the commonwealth. Stories of audacity, recklessness, greed and ride over the common man. It is challenging the people on what they can

item.title: At UN, Cuba rallies support against ‘ruthless’ US blockade
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Diplomatic tensions between Washington and Havana have widened in recent months.

item.title: Indonesia Honors Modi With Top Civilian Honor
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi received Indonesia's highest civilian honor, the Bintang Adipurna, during his visit to Jakarta. The award brings his total number of honors from foreign governments to at least 35. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: German-speaking students in Nigeria face less competition for opportunities- German official
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: In Nigeria’s crowded job market, the low supply of German speaking graduates positions them favourably for global careers. Speaking exclusively read more German-speaking students in Nigeria face less competition for opportunities- German official

item.title: Better deal for indigenous sailors
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Nigeria cannot keep applauding its seafarers once a year and then abandoning them to harsh working realities at sea. The renewed call for better welfare is timely because, without sailors, there is no maritime trade, and without maritime trade, the wider economy suffers. The demand for improved welf

item.title: Should government offices be privatised? By Usman Sarki
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: “The phrase, public office is a public trust, has of late become common property“, Charles Sumner The Code of Conduct Bureau  rightly admonishes Nigerians that “Public office is a trust, don’t abuse it!” In the same spirit, Article XI, Section 1 of the 1987 Constitution of the Republic of

item.title: State Police: Tinubu Inaugurates Panel To Draft National Policing Bill
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu on Tuesday inaugurated the Presidential Working Group on the National Policing Bill to prepare the legal framework for the implementation of state police across the country. President Tinubu, represented by his Chief of Staff, Mr Femi Gbajabiamila, inaugurated the panel a

item.title: Childhood on hold: How Gaza’s children spend a summer without play
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Gaza mothers describe a third wartime summer where children carry adult burdens, deprived of play and art.

item.title: NNPC signs 6 gas deals to drive industrial revital, energy security
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Nigerian National Petroleum Company Limited (NNPC) has signed six strategic agreements with key industry partners aimed at deepening gas read more NNPC signs 6 gas deals to drive industrial revital, energy security

item.title: Uganda: High Court Orders Inquest Into 2018 Killing of Bobi Wine's Driver Yasin Kawuma
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Nile Post] KAMPALA -- The High Court has ordered a formal judicial inquest into the 2018 shooting of Yasin Kawuma, the driver of opposition leader Robert Kyagulanyi, popularly known as Bobi Wine, paving the way for a public inquiry into one of Uganda's most politically contentious unresolved killin

item.title: Rise of Hausa irredentism, by Ochereome Nnanna
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Northern Nigeria has been the nation’s Achilles Heel. Yes, they produce the bulk of the food consumed in Nigeria. That, in itself, is an anomaly which is tied to the South’s greater urban orientation and consequential retreat from farming. The nation largely depends on Northern Nigeria for much of f

item.title: How Mismanagement, Policy Failures Destroyed 51 Northern Companies
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: …Kaduna Textiles, Arewa Breweries, NISUCO Among Casualties …Communities Left With Job Losses, Poverty, Abandoned Plants At least 51 industries across northern Nigeria have shut down over the past three decades, with labour activists, former workers and industry stakeholders attributing the collapse

item.title: Syria grapples with unrest as government faces old and new threats
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Damascus shaken by twin blasts during Macron's visit, marking heightened threats to Syria's fragile stability

item.title: Ministry, Stakeholders Meet, Woo Investors To Industrial, Commercial Expo
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Federal Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security (FMAFS) has held an inter-ministerial stakeholders’ meeting in Abuja to prepare for the industrial and commercial Expo Abuja 2026. The parley was used to strengthen collaboration to drive industrialisation, trade, agribusiness and investment in

item.title: US Strikes Iran, Blocks Oil Sales | The Asia Trade 7/8/2026
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global

item.title: Almajiri Commission Distances Self From Disputed Budget Allocations
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Commission for Almajiri and Out-of-School Children’s Education (NCAOOSCE) has distanced itself from controversial projects contained in the 2026 Appropriation Act. The commission stated that the items were not initiated by the agency but were National Assembly constituency projects assi

item.title: Toriola’s take on MTN and the telco ecosystem, by Okoh Aihe
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Sometimes truth can be too acerbic to be accommodated by reality. It hits hard in the face to the point of irritation while others can simply sneer at a point of profundity which, of course, doesn’t look very popular. Listening to the MTN Nigeria boss, Karl Toriola, speak recently about his organisa

item.title: The curious case of Gbaja and the Prince, by Rotimi Fasan
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: If presidential spokesperson Bayo Onanuga is to be believed, the so called director, Adeyemi Adeniyi Matthew, of a so called Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council or PFIPC should appear in court in about three weeks from now. That is on July 27 to answer multiple charges of impersonati

item.title: Federal Govt Unveils Flood Protection Scheme For Most Vulnerable States
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Federal government has taken steps to reduce the risk of flooding through its Disaster Risk Reduction Strategy, following a review of the 2026 Annual Flood Outlook. The director-general of the National Emergency Management Agency (NEMA), Mrs Zubaida Umar Abubakar, disclosed this yesterday in Abuja d

item.title: Zimbabwe: Mnangagwa Signs Constitutional Amendment Bill Into Law
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [New Zimbabwe] PRESIDENT Emmerson Mnangagwa has signed the Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 into law, effectively extending his second term and rewarding parliamentarians with an extra two years.

item.title: El-Rufai’s wife appeals to Tinubu over ‘dehumanising’ detention
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Asiya El-Rufai, the wife of former Kaduna State Governor Nasir El-Rufai has launched a direct, emotional appeal to President Bola read more El-Rufai’s wife appeals to Tinubu over ‘dehumanising’ detention

item.title: North-Central agenda for presidential candidates ahead 2027
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The emergence of presidential contenders on the platforms of various political parties for the 2027 presidential elections has spurred agitation and demands about what Nigerians want to be prioritised by whoever wins the election. The post North-Central agenda for presidential candidates ahead 2027

item.title: Tankers Trickle Through Hormuz After US Strikes Iran
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Only a handful of oil carriers appeared to transit through the Strait of Hormuz early on Wednesday, even after a spate of strikes on ships rattled owners. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski breaks down the factors at play as ship owners navigate heightened tensions. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Unusual or predictable? How Europe is dominating the World Cup
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Six of the eight quarter-finalists at the World Cup in North America are from Europe, so why are they doing so well and what does history tell us about the chances of one of them winning it all?

item.title: Argentina fan waves Israeli flag at World Cup match with Egypt
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: An Argentina fan waves the Israeli flag during his team’s Round of 16 match against Egypt.

item.title: ATIDI CEO Manuel Moses targets $2bn to unlock Africa infrastructure
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Instead of building physical bridges, former engineer Manuel Moses now designs the financial architecture to unlock billions for Africa’s infrastructure as head of its largest multilateral risk insurer.

item.title: President Tinubu Orders ICPC To Investigate PFIPC Allegations
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has directed the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission to conduct a thorough investigation into the activities of a group calling itself the Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council and all related matters.The President ordered tha

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: An “All Support Groups for President Bola Ahmed Tinubu” has been formed under the All Progressives Congress (APC) as part of a volunteer network to unify a coalition dedicated to securing 27 million votes and a decisive mandate for the the President in next year’s election. The nat

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Tech
item.description: Flutterwave has secured a strategic investment from Circle Ventures, the investment arm of New York Stock Exchange-listed Circle Internet Group, read more Flutterwave’s Circle deal signals stablecoins are moving from crypto to core payment infrastructure

item.title: Katsina Activist Defends Remi Tinubu’s Empowerment Drive
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A Katsina-based women’s activist and microcredit expert, Hajia Zainab Muhammad Sada, has defended First Lady Oluremi Tinubu over her recent call for Nigerian women to embrace low-capital businesses such as akara frying, corn roasting and kuli-kuli production, describing the criticism trailing

item.title: Soybean Oil Hits Three-Week High on Renewed US, Iran Tensions
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Chicago soybean oil futures climbed to a three-week high after fresh US military strikes on Iran sent crude oil prices higher, raising the prospect of greater demand for biofuel feedstocks.

item.title: Nigeria Records 65,759 Suspected Diphtheria Cases, 2,229 Deaths
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria has recorded 65,759 suspected cases of diphtheria and 2,229 deaths since the outbreak began in 2022, underscoring the continued threat posed by the vaccine-preventable disease despite recent gains in containment efforts. The latest figures, released by the Nigeria Centre for Disease Control

item.title: Sudan: UN Condemns RSF Escalation Around El Obeid As Tasees Backs Ceasefire
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Dabanga] El Obeid / El Fasher / Geneva / Chad -- The UN Human Rights Council has unanimously condemned the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) for escalating violence in and around El Obeid, warning that the North Kordofan capital faces a growing risk of mass atrocities, while the RSF-aligned S

item.title: EFCC Hands Over Recovered Assets Worth Billions Of Naira To Education Ministry
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has handed over recovered assets worth billions of naira forfeited to the federal government to the Federal Ministry of Education as part of efforts to channel proceeds of crime into national development. The items, presented during a ceremony at t

item.title: Khamenei’s coffin arrives in Iraq as US strikes hit Iran
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The coffin carrying Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei has arrived in one of Shia Islam’s holiest cities, Najaf.

item.title: NNPC: $3.4bn Saved Through Contract Restructuring, N19.5tn Remitted to Federation in 14 Months
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • Maintains 100% JV cash call payments, seeks partners’ compliance • FG has hired PwC to streamline over 270 fees, taxes, says Lokpobiri • Renaissance finds new oil in OML

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • Panel will evolve implementation-ready draft National Policing Bill for transmission to the N’Assembly Deji Elumoye in Abuja President Bola Tinubu has inaugurated a seven-man Presidential Working Group on the

item.title: Tinubu to Afreximbank: Speed Up Move to Mobilise Resources for Africa’s Industrialisation
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • Bank commits $20bn into Nigerian economy in the last five years Deji Elumoye in Abuja President Bola Tinubu has charged the leadership of African Export-Import Bank (Afreximbank) to intensify

item.title: Atiku Ally Dumps ADC For APC, Says Coalition Is A ‘Hoax’
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A political ally of former Vice President Atiku Abubakar, Dr Aslam Aliyu, has resigned from the African Democratic Congress (ADC) and defected to the ruling All Progressives Congress (APC), describing the proposed opposition coalition as a “hoax.” ALiyu announced her defection in a state

item.title: Congo-Kinshasa: Ebola Continues to Spread in DR Congo As Death Toll Passes 500, WHO Warns
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [UN News] The outbreak of the deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is expanding, while the push to accelerate testing and identify effective treatment options continues, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) said on Tuesday.

item.title: Court Declares ARCON’s N60bn Fine Against Facebook Nigeria Illegal
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Justice Yellim Bogoro of the Federal High Court in Lagos has declared the N60 billion fine imposed by the Advertising Regulatory Council of Nigeria (ARCON) on Facebook Nigeria Operations Limited Illegal, stating that the regulator exceeded its legal authority and breached the company’s constit

item.title: South Sudan: South Sudan At 15 - How the Political Elite Have Found a Way to Profit From Peace As Well As War
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [The Conversation Africa] South Sudan's independence from Sudan in 2011 was meant to close the chapter on one of Africa's longest civil wars: the north-south war that preceded it. Formally, it did. But independence did not end the deeper struggles over power, revenue and coercion inside the newly in

item.title: Reps Summon Accountant-General, Six MDAs Over Failure to Honour Invitations
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Juliet Akoje in Abuja House of Representatives Public Accounts Committee has expressed displeasure over what it described as the non-responsive attitude of Accountant-General of the Federation (AGF), Mr. Shamseldeen Babatunde

item.title: Reducing NCAA’s Revenue Dangerous to Aviation Safety, Agency Warns
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kasim Sumaina in Abuja Nigeria Civil Aviation Authority (NCAA) has warned that any move to reduce its statutory revenue could undermine aviation safety by weakening its capacity to carry out

item.title: UTM FLNG, NNPC, Seplat Sign 15-Year Gas Sale Deal for 200mmscf/d

item.description: • Agreement expected to pave way for FID in Q4 2026 • Project to monetise stranded gas, boost LNG exports and industrialisation Emmanuel Addeh in Abuja Nigeria moved a step

item.description: Nume Ekeghe Zedvance Finance Limited has announced the appointment of Professor Pius ‘Deji Olanrewaju as Chairman of its Board of Directors, with effect from July 1, 2026, pending regulatory approval

item.description: • Shettima, Otti, UNDP Launch Nigeria’s First Manu-Tech UniPod at MOUAU, Umudike Boniface Okoro in Umuahia and Kuni Tyessi in Abuja The federal government says its vision is to transform

item.title: US and Iran trade strikes after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The US military says it hit missile sites and command centres while Iran says it attacked US bases.

item.title: Big Market Rotation Sends Korean Stocks Tumbling, China Surging
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The AI rotation trade is gathering pace in Asia as investors pull money from the chipmakers that powered this year’s rally and hunt for cheaper ways to play the technology boom.
